# Act 2: Communication Artifacts {.section .learning-section}

<p class="section-subtitle">How shared meaning becomes visible, transferable, and actionable.</p>

---

## 09. From Problems to Desired Outcomes

<p class="slide-kicker">Protopipe does not jump from pain to solution. It defines what must become true.</p>

<div class="outcome-map">
<div class="outcome-card">
<span>CEO</span>
<strong>Simple decisions through evidence</strong>
Management decisions become simpler because evidence is accessible, reliable, and safe to surface.
</div>

<div class="outcome-card">
<span>CFO</span>
<strong>KPIs built in</strong>
Impact, constraints, and opportunity cost become visible early enough to steer investment.
</div>

<div class="outcome-card">
<span>CPO</span>
<strong>Hypotheses instead of roadmap defense</strong>
Product decisions become explicit assumptions with signals and revisit criteria.
</div>

<div class="outcome-card">
<span>CTO</span>
<strong>Scale change, not just throughput</strong>
Architecture localizes change, risk, and understanding as the organization grows.
</div>
</div>

---

## 10. Mechanics Make Outcomes Unavoidable

<p class="slide-kicker">Mechanics are enforced trade-offs. They remove comfortable failure modes.</p>

<div class="mechanic-chain">
<div class="mechanic-step">Problem</div>
<div class="mechanic-arrow">→</div>
<div class="mechanic-step">Desired Outcome</div>
<div class="mechanic-arrow">→</div>
<div class="mechanic-step learning">Mechanic</div>
<div class="mechanic-arrow">→</div>
<div class="mechanic-step">Contextual Decision</div>
<div class="mechanic-arrow">→</div>
<div class="mechanic-step">Realization</div>
</div>

<div class="mechanic-punchline">
If a rule can be ignored without consequence, it is not a mechanic.
</div>

<div class="mechanic-type-grid">
<div class="mechanic-type-card">
<span>X over Y</span>
Use when a mechanic enforces a clear preference
between two competing approaches.

<i>Y is excluded by default. It needs strong justification to be chosen over X.</i>
</div>

<div class="mechanic-type-card">
<span>X by Default, Y by Exception</span>
Use when a behavior is allowed,
but only with explicit justification.

<i>Y is allowed, but only for the given exception</i>
</div>

<div class="mechanic-type-card">
<span>No X without Y</span>
Use when a behavior must never occur
unless a prerequisite is fulfilled.

<i>X is not allowed unless Y is true</i>
</div>
</div>

---

## 11. The First Mechanics

<p class="slide-kicker">A shared reality platform starts by making ambiguity structurally expensive.</p>

<div class="mechanic-grid">
<div class="mechanic-card">
<strong>No coordination without explicit shared meaning</strong>
Coordination through meetings alone does not scale. Meaning must be referenceable.
</div>

<div class="mechanic-card">
<strong>No release without observable feedback</strong>
Delivery is not complete until feedback exists before or during release.
</div>

<div class="mechanic-card">
<strong>No work evaluation without learning signal</strong>
Work is evaluated by learning, not by looking right or wrong.
</div>

<div class="mechanic-card">
<strong>No experiment without explicit business ownership</strong>
Experiments need accountable interpretation, not just technical execution.
</div>
</div>

---

## 12. Gapless Organizational Learning Process

<p class="slide-kicker">Purpose, strategy, delivery, observation, and learning stay in one measurable loop.</p>

```{=html}
<div class="enterprise-bpm">
<img class="enterprise-bpmn-img" src="../assets/diagrams/shared-reality-bpmn.svg" alt="BPMN-style shared reality process across C-Level, Product Ownership, Developers and Analysts, and Learning lanes; analysis combines experiment metrics with external factors such as weather">
</div>
```

<div class="shared-reality-punchline">
Protopipe exists to keep this loop connected, explicit, observable, and revisable.
</div>

---

## 13. Process Transition Artifacts

<p class="slide-kicker">Every handover becomes visible as a shared artifact that the next lane can understand and act on.</p>

<div class="artifact-flow">
<div class="artifact-card">
<span>Purpose</span>
<strong>Golden Circle</strong>
Why, how, and what become a stable reference for strategic and product conversations.
<em>Visible as shared orientation.</em>
</div>

<div class="artifact-card focus">
<span>Strategy</span>
<strong>Strategy Topic + KPI Tree</strong>
The KPI Tree relates operational and technical metrics to the business outcomes that matter to leadership.
<em>Visible as causal business context.</em>
</div>

<div class="artifact-card">
<span>Hypothesis</span>
<strong>Strategy-linked Claim</strong>
A hypothesis belongs to a strategy topic and states what should become measurably true.
<em>Visible as strategic assumption.</em>
</div>

<div class="artifact-card experiment">
<span>Experiment</span>
<strong>Measured System Change</strong>
An experiment tries to prove a hypothesis by changing or replacing part of the current system and expecting metric movement.
<em>Visible as portfolio experiment.</em>
</div>

<div class="artifact-card">
<span>Development</span>
<strong>Feature Files / Scenarios</strong>
Instead of story points, progress is visible as missing scenario steps on the experiment branch.
<em>Visible on the experiment.</em>
</div>
</div>

<div class="artifact-punchline">
Artifacts are not write-only documentation. They are the shared objects that carry responsibility across the process.
</div>
